articles.title: Team Management Best Practices
articles.whenToRead: When managing Team tab, configuring team orchestration, or delegating tasks
articles.contentMd:
# Team Management Best Practices

## Delegating Tasks
- Use `team-task` fenced code blocks in chat to delegate work to the Team pipeline
- Each task must be self-contained ŌĆö the developer agent has no chat context
- One task = one logical change. Do not merge unrelated features
- Include: what to change, why, affected files/services, constraints
- Set priority (lower number = higher priority, default 100)
- **NEVER write directly to backlog.json** ŌĆö always use `team-task` code blocks

## Task Format
````
```team-task
{"rawIdea": "Full task description", "priority": 10}
```
````

## Task Quality
- Write clear, specific task descriptions ŌĆö vague tasks produce vague results
- Include acceptance criteria when possible
- Reference specific file paths and function names
- Mention any constraints (no new dependencies, backwards compatibility, etc.)

## Monitoring
- Use the Team Status script (Ctrl+Shift+T) to check orchestrator state
- Monitor the Team tab pipeline: Ideas ŌåÆ Analysis ŌåÆ Planning ŌåÆ Approval ŌåÆ Backlog ŌåÆ Queue ŌåÆ Completed
- Watch for stalled tasks ŌĆö they may need clarification or breaking into smaller pieces

## When NOT to Delegate
- Architectural decisions requiring user input ŌĆö discuss first, then delegate implementation
- Quick single-file fixes ŌĆö faster to do directly
- Tasks requiring interactive exploration ŌĆö the team agent works autonomously
- Cross-project changes ŌĆö team works within one project directory

articles.title: Figma Design-to-Code
articles.whenToRead: When user shares a Figma link, asks to implement a design from Figma, or mentions Figma API token
articles.contentMd:
# Figma Design-to-Code

Uses MCP server `figma-developer-mcp` (registry: `io.github.GLips/Figma-Context-MCP`).

## Tools
- **`get_figma_data`** ŌĆö get layout, styles, content from a frame
  - `fileKey` (required): from URL `figma.com/(file|design)/<fileKey>/...`
  - `nodeId` (optional): from URL param `node-id=`, format `1234:5678`. Always pass if present
  - `depth` (optional): tree traversal depth. Do NOT use unless user explicitly asks
- **`download_figma_images`** ŌĆö download SVG/PNG assets
  - `fileKey`, `nodes` (array of {nodeId, fileName, imageRef}), `localPath`, `pngScale` (default 2x)

## Authentication
- **Figma Personal Access Token** ŌĆö created in Figma ŌåÆ Settings ŌåÆ Personal Access Tokens
- Token expires every **90 days** ŌĆö remind user to renew if calls fail with 403
- Stored in MCP config (`~/.claude.json`) as env var `FIGMA_API_KEY`

## Token Renewal
```bash
claude mcp remove figma-context-mcp
claude mcp add -e FIGMA_API_KEY='NEW_TOKEN' -s user figma-context-mcp -- npx -y figma-developer-mcp
```

## Best Practices
- Links to specific frames/groups give better results than whole file links
- Start from the outermost container, then drill into child components
- Download images/icons as SVG when possible, PNG for photos

articles.title: Working with Excel & CSV Files
articles.whenToRead: When user asks to read, create, or modify Excel (.xlsx/.xls) or CSV files
articles.contentMd:
# Working with Excel & CSV Files

## Reading CSV
- **Small files**: Use the Read tool directly ŌĆö CSV is plain text
- **Analysis/filtering**: Use Python: `import csv` (built-in, no pip install needed)
- **PowerShell**: `Import-Csv 'file.csv'` for quick inspection

## Reading Excel (.xlsx)
- **Python** (preferred): `pip install openpyxl` then `openpyxl.load_workbook('file.xlsx')`
- **PowerShell**: `Import-Excel` module (may need `Install-Module ImportExcel`)
- Claude's Read tool does NOT natively read .xlsx ŌĆö use Python/PowerShell

## Creating Excel
```python
from openpyxl import Workbook
wb = Workbook()
ws = wb.active
ws.title = 'Sheet1'
ws.append(['Header1', 'Header2'])  # header row
ws.append(['value1', 'value2'])     # data row
wb.save('output.xlsx')
```

## Creating CSV
```python
import csv
with open('output.csv', 'w', newline='') as f:
    writer = csv.writer(f)
    writer.writerow(['Header1', 'Header2'])
    writer.writerow(['value1', 'value2'])
```

## Key Tips
- Always check if Python & openpyxl are available before using: `python3 -c "import openpyxl"` or `pip install openpyxl`
- For large datasets, prefer `csv` over `openpyxl` (lower memory)
- When converting xlsxŌåÆcsv, handle multiple sheets
- Preserve encoding: use `encoding='utf-8-sig'` for Excel compatibility

articles.title: Working with Word Documents
articles.whenToRead: When user asks to read, create, or modify Word (.docx) documents
articles.contentMd:
# Working with Word Documents (.docx)

## Reading
- Claude's Read tool does NOT natively read .docx
- **Python**: `pip install python-docx` then read paragraphs/tables
- **Quick text extract**: `python3 -c "from docx import Document; [print(p.text) for p in Document('file.docx').paragraphs]"`

## Creating
```python
from docx import Document
from docx.shared import Pt, Inches

doc = Document()
doc.add_heading('Title', level=0)
doc.add_paragraph('Body text here.')

# Table
table = doc.add_table(rows=2, cols=3, style='Table Grid')
table.cell(0, 0).text = 'Header'

doc.save('output.docx')
```

## Formatting
- **Bold/italic**: `run = p.add_run('text'); run.bold = True`
- **Font size**: `run.font.size = Pt(12)`
- **Page break**: `doc.add_page_break()`
- **Image**: `doc.add_picture('image.png', width=Inches(4))`

## Key Tips
- Always check: `pip install python-docx` (package name is `python-docx`, import is `docx`)
- For templates: open existing .docx, find/replace placeholders, save as new file
- Tables: use `style='Table Grid'` for visible borders
- Don't confuse with `docx2txt` (read-only) ŌĆö `python-docx` can both read and write

articles.title: Working with PDF Files
articles.whenToRead: When user asks to read, create, merge, or manipulate PDF files
articles.contentMd:
# Working with PDF Files

## Reading PDF
- **Claude's Read tool reads PDFs natively** ŌĆö just use `Read` with the file path
- For large PDFs (>10 pages): use `pages` parameter, e.g. `pages: "1-5"`
- Max 20 pages per Read request

## Extracting Text Programmatically
```python
# pip install pdfplumber (best for tables + text)
import pdfplumber
with pdfplumber.open('file.pdf') as pdf:
    for page in pdf.pages:
        print(page.extract_text())
        tables = page.extract_tables()  # structured table data
```

## Creating PDF
```python
# pip install reportlab
from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import A4
from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

c = canvas.Canvas('output.pdf', pagesize=A4)
c.setFont('Helvetica', 12)
c.drawString(72, 750, 'Hello World')
c.save()
```

## Merging / Splitting
```python
# pip install pypdf
from pypdf import PdfReader, PdfWriter
writer = PdfWriter()
for pdf_file in ['file1.pdf', 'file2.pdf']:
    writer.append(pdf_file)
writer.write('merged.pdf')
```

## Key Tips
- **Read first with Read tool** ŌĆö no Python needed for inspection
- For complex layouts (forms, signatures): use `pypdf` or `pikepdf`
- For HTMLŌåÆPDF conversion: `pip install weasyprint` or `wkhtmltopdf`
- Always check available Python packages before installing

articles.title: Windows Shell Safety Rules
articles.whenToRead: Always relevant ŌĆö critical rules for running shell commands on Windows
articles.contentMd:
# Windows Shell Safety

## /dev/null Ban
**NEVER** use `/dev/null` in Bash commands on Windows (e.g. `2>/dev/null`, `> /dev/null`). This creates a literal file named `nul` which breaks cloud sync (OneDrive, Dropbox). Instead:
- Suppress stderr: `2>&1` (merge streams)
- Ignore errors: `|| true`
- Discard output: pipe to a temp file and delete, or restructure the command

## Path Conventions
- Use forward slashes in bash: `/c/Users/...` not `C:\Users\...`
- Quote paths with spaces: `"path with spaces/file.txt"`
- Git Bash translates `/c/` to `C:\` automatically

## Common Pitfalls
- `find` command: Windows `find` ŌēĀ Unix `find`. Use Glob/Grep tools instead
- `rm -rf`: works in Git Bash but be careful with OneDrive paths
- Line endings: Windows uses CRLF. Use `git config core.autocrlf true`
- `nul` file: if already created, delete manually via File Explorer (not bash)

articles.title: CCW Keyboard Shortcuts Reference
articles.whenToRead: When user asks about keyboard shortcuts or navigation
articles.contentMd:
# CCW Keyboard Shortcuts

## Chat
- **Ctrl+Enter** ŌĆö Send message
- **Escape** ŌĆö Cancel current processing
- **Ctrl+N** ŌĆö New session
- **Ctrl+V** ŌĆö Paste screenshot from clipboard
- **Drag and drop** ŌĆö Attach files
- **Ctrl+Shift+T** ŌĆö Team Status (script)

## IDE / Code Editor
- **Ctrl+S** ŌĆö Save file
- **Ctrl+F** ŌĆö Find in file
- **Ctrl+H** ŌĆö Find and replace
- **Ctrl+G** ŌĆö Go to line
- **Ctrl+Space** ŌĆö IntelliSense completion

articles.title: Useful Claude CLI Flags
articles.whenToRead: When user asks about CLI configuration, token limits, model selection, or advanced CLI features
articles.contentMd:
# Useful Claude CLI Flags (not yet used by CCW)

Verified via `claude --help` (v2.1.62). These flags could be useful for advanced users:

## High Priority
- `--effort <low|medium|high>` ŌĆö thinking effort level (token savings)
- `--max-budget-usd <N>` ŌĆö spending cap per session
- `--fallback-model <model>` ŌĆö resilience: fallback if primary model unavailable
- `--permission-mode <mode>` ŌĆö 5 modes: acceptEdits, bypassPermissions, default, dontAsk, plan

## Medium Priority
- `--fork-session` ŌĆö conversation branching
- `--add-dir <path>` ŌĆö expand workspace beyond working directory
- `--allowedTools / --disallowedTools` ŌĆö granular tool control
- `--debug-file <path>` ŌĆö diagnostic output to file
- `--system-prompt / --append-system-prompt` ŌĆö customizable instructions

## Low Priority
- `--agents / --agent` ŌĆö custom agent definitions
- `--mcp-config` ŌĆö MCP server configuration file
- `--file` ŌĆö startup resources
